tier_remaining_note: Tier A remains fully cleared (0). Tier B recomputed from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model on 2026-07-21 (ROUTES_DIR fixed locally to the non-Windows path for this run only, the script file on disk still has the Windows-style path and needs a permanent fix): 383 remaining before chunk 91, minus the 50 Tier B pairs chunk 91 built, leaves 333. Not yet covered by verify_build_state.py's automatic disk reconciliation (only route_pairs/blog/total_markdown_files are); re-run the generator manually each chunk until that gap is closed.
notes: Chunk 91 DONE (Template A, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 7 parallel research agents, one per cluster, taking the 50 highest-scored unbuilt Tier B pairs from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model (6,821 built, Tier A=0, Tier B=383 remaining before this chunk). UAE outbound cluster (17 routes): united-arab-emirates-to-X for Bosnia and Herzegovina, Cameroon, Eswatini, Ivory Coast, Kosovo, Kyrgyzstan, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Mozambique, North Macedonia, Senegal, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan. Destination-side import_requirements reused (reworded, not copied) from each country's already-verified united-kingdom-to-X/united-states-to-X sibling pages; the new research was UAE's own MOCCAE export side (moccae.gov.ae export certificate service, AED 100 fee, UAE Pass login) plus fresh airline routing per destination. Germany outbound cluster (5 routes): germany-to-brunei/laos/seychelles/trinidad-and-tobago/uzbekistan, same reuse pattern for destination import rules, fresh EU/BVL export-side research and germany-specific airline routings (e.g. Uzbekistan Airways Frankfurt-Tashkent 3x/week, Vietnam Airlines Frankfurt-Hanoi daily). Singapore reciprocal cluster (8 routes, both directions): Dominican Republic, Jamaica, Kazakhstan, Maldives. Corrected a stale site-wide terminology gap: Singapore's AVS import system has moved from the old Category A/B/C/D naming (still used in data/countries_pet_regulations.json and several older sibling pages) to Schedule I/II/III, confirmed by live-fetching avs.nparks.gov.sg on 21 July 2026; none of the four destination countries appear on Schedule I or II, so all four are Schedule III (WOAH titre test, 30-day AQC quarantine). Three brand-new-country clusters (20 routes total, first-ever site coverage): Antigua and Barbuda and Gabon to/from UK+US (6 routes); Grenada and Libya to/from UK+US (6 routes); Papua New Guinea, Saint Lucia and Sudan to/from UK+US (8 routes, UK-to-Sudan and all US-reverse-legs deliberately left for a future chunk to keep the batch at 50). CDC high-risk-for-dog-rabies status and UK DEFRA/APHA Part 1/Part 2 listing were independently verified per country via the CDC's live high-risk list (updated 15 April 2026) and the EU Regulation 577/2013 Annex II list the UK's retained law still uses, not assumed: Gabon, Libya and Sudan confirmed CDC high-risk; Antigua and Barbuda, Grenada, Papua New Guinea and Saint Lucia confirmed not high-risk; Antigua and Barbuda and Saint Lucia confirmed UK Part 2 listed (no titre test); Gabon, Grenada, Libya and Papua New Guinea confirmed unlisted (titre test plus 90-day wait applies). Honest uncertainty flagged rather than guessed on two pages where current source material is genuinely thin: Libya's export process (two rival administrations, Mitiga airport's fluctuating status) and Sudan's export process and airline routing (Khartoum airport only reopened to scheduled international flights on 12 February 2026 after nearly three years closed). Other genuine differentiators found: flydubai's Dubai-Skopje route has been terminated despite still appearing in some search tools; Somon Air explicitly refuses animals on its only Dushanbe-Dubai sector; Air Niugini restarted Port Moresby-Tokyo Narita on 18 July 2026, days before this chunk's date; Singapore Airlines flies the only true nonstop pairing in the Singapore cluster (SIN-MLE, 14x weekly) though Maldives law bars dog imports outright so it's a cats-only route. QA pass after the agents finished: zero em dashes, en dashes or curly quotes across all 50 files (verified with a Unicode grep sweep); zero banned vocabulary (one false-positive 'PetEmbark'-contains-'embark' string caught and confirmed harmless on re-check); correct Marcus Webb persona and template_variant A on all 50; every export_requirements/import_requirements block verified as a keyed YAML map via a Python parser, never a list; route_complexity restricted to the valid low/moderate/high/very_high tokens; zero corpus-wide title/description collisions checked against the full 6,871-file corpus; government-authority contact emails/phone numbers on destination-country pages (e.g. Antigua's Veterinary and Livestock Division, Grenada's Ministry of Agriculture) are the cited regulatory source, not a PetTransportGlobal contact channel, consistent with prior chunks' sourcing style. Routes 6,821 -> 6,871, pages 7,627 -> 7,677. Chunk 92 (Template B, Tier B) is next.
_previous_notes_chunk_90: Chunk 90 DONE (Template E, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 8 parallel research agents, one per country/hub cluster. Australia/UAE reciprocal cluster (36 routes): closed the reverse legs for the 18 countries chunk 89 opened one-direction-only. Turkmenistan cluster (3 routes). New Macau hub cluster (6 routes, brand-new pairs): Hong Kong, Japan and South Africa both directions with Macau. UAE reverse-leg cluster (5 routes). Key finds: DAFF non-approved countries each picked a source-justified staging country; Montenegro confirmed DAFF Group 3 and MOCCAE low-risk, a genuine lighter outlier; MOCCAE's post-titre wait cross-checked against USDA APHIS's own UAE page (no fixed wait); flydubai/Etihad/Somon Air/Air Macau carrier exclusions found fresh. Two pre-existing sibling-page issues flagged, not fixed: several Australia-bound pages wrongly imply Group 3 skips the 180-day wait and that Perth is a valid PEQ landing point; Kosovo/Kyrgyzstan/Lesotho UK/US pages link to non-existent /pet-transport/origins/ pages. Routes 6,771 -> 6,821, pages 7,577 -> 7,627. Chunk 91 (Template A, Tier B) was next.
_previous_notes_chunk_89: Chunk 89 DONE (Template D, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 7 parallel research agents, one per cluster. Iceland cluster (8 routes): both directions with Hong Kong, Japan, South Africa and South Korea. Canada cluster (14 routes): both directions with Barbados, Belize, Botswana, Guyana, Namibia, Suriname and Zambia. Turkmenistan (1 route): the missing united-states-to-turkmenistan leg. Albania/Algeria/Angola cluster (9 routes): both directions to Australia plus the outbound leg to the UAE for each. Australia export cluster (18 routes, Australia-to-X one direction only): Bahamas, Belarus, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Cameroon, Eswatini, Ivory Coast, Kosovo, Kyrgyzstan, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Mozambique, North Macedonia, Senegal and Tajikistan. Key finds: MAST places Japan in Category 1 but leaves Hong Kong, South Africa and South Korea unclassified; Japan's MAFF treats Iceland as a designated region; CFIA's high-risk list clears Barbados but lists the other six Canada-cluster countries; DAFF confirmed Albania/Algeria/Angola non-approved, each with a source-justified staging country. Routes 6,721 -> 6,771, pages 7,527 -> 7,577. Chunk 90 (Template E, Tier B) was next.

counts.note: total_site_pages counts content .md source files. The full deployed total (including Hugo taxonomy and list pages) is verified from sitemap.xml after a build.

counts_method: Reconciled from disk by verify_build_state.py. Never hand-edit count fields; run 'python verify_build_state.py --write' instead.
_dropped_from_rolling_window: Chunks 80, 81, 82-83, 84, 85-86, 87 and 88 notes were dropped to cap file growth (git history still has them in full at commits 4ecac2f1, 3741f752, ef4d776e, ca50c52f, bd68e3c5, 0cac6bc3 and 6bb8b070). A rolling window of the two most recent previous-chunk notes (currently chunk 90 and chunk 89) is kept going forward.
